blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

# Load counts and metadata
counts <- read.csv('counts_de.csv', row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv('metadata_de.csv', row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

# Ensure counts are integer matrix
counts <- as.matrix(counts)
storage.mode(counts) <- 'integer'

cat("Counts matrix:", dim(counts), "(genes × cells)\n")
cat("Metadata:\n")
print(table(metadata$cell_type))

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                               colData = metadata,
                               design = ~ cell_type)

cat("\nDESeqDataSet created:\n")
print(dds)

# Filter: genes with >= 10 counts in >= 3 cells
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 3
dds <- dds[keep, ]
cat(f"\nAfter filtering (>= 10 counts in >= 3 cells):\n")
cat(f"  Genes retained: {nrow(dds)}\n")
